title: School-WASH hygiene-promotion pack
context: School-WASH hygiene promotion is an important channel for behavior change, reaching children and through them households. This backlog task is partner/context-driven: the content pack's topics, format, and age-appropriate framing should be guided by a secured partner who works in school-health or education. It is medium-risk (hygiene promotion, not clinical or dosing content) but requires WASH domain review and culturally appropriate framing for school settings.
objective: Author, source-cite, and get domain-reviewed a school-WASH hygiene-promotion content pack covering key protective behaviors (handwashing, safe water use, latrine use/maintenance, menstrual hygiene management in school) in age-appropriate, plain-language format suitable for distribution in school settings.
acceptanceCriteria: Content pack covers key school-WASH protective behaviors: handwashing at school, safe water practices, latrine/toilet use and hygiene, and MHH for school-age girls where appropriate
Content is age-appropriate for school settings (format, vocabulary, and step complexity appropriate for children and adolescents)
Every claim tied to a named authoritative source with retrieval date and verified license; no verbatim copied text; no logos/emblems; CI copy/similarity check passes
Unit conforms to the WashUnit schema: riskClass is medium, no clinical instruction, reviewed and approved by a WASH/public-health domain reviewer distinct from the author
If a school-WASH partner drives the context, their requirements and language preferences are reflected; PR-tied review-log entry written
CI schema validation, citation-coverage, no-self-approval, readability, and copy/similarity gates all pass
output: A committed, domain-reviewed, source-cited school-WASH hygiene-promotion content pack covering key protective behaviors in age-appropriate format, with a PR-tied review-log entry.
